review_title: Good Software, maybe exatly waht you need.
description: It's good software. There are things they can improve to be a reference, there aren't a lot of user options out there.
author: Erick D.
pros: It's very easy to use and most of the features you need to work on QA with (or without) a team are here. Filters, priority, easy to put images, enter device details and you can customize the interface to your bug report!
cons: There are some interface decisions that I didn't like: in the board view I couldn't delete my cards; I would prefer horizontal scrolling to be visible full-time (without having to scroll to the bottom of the page to see it). If you work alone on a project, there is no need for the "My Dashboard" area. When a Bug is created it automatically shows "open" and sometimes this is not real. A bug is opened when it is ready to be fixed (tested as a bug), the software may provide some backlog, such as a "waiting" status.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

cons: None as of now. Will update.............

review_title: A smart workspace for defect management
author: Sachin S.
pros: Very few tools like Bugasura have focused on user experience over anything else and that makes Bugasura good for me and my team. Easy, efficient, and smart mechanism to report defect both in quick manner as well as detailed one. The best part I like is how the entire thread of communication (comments) are maintained as those are vital details from defect detection till defect resolution and keep them to refer in future is central for a defect management process. Dashboard is again an amazing piece of work done by the tool creators it give entire picture at a glance ( per customizations) in very intuitive fashion. Needless to mention that the App of Bugasura is very nice and enhances the ease of ensuring the efficiency and availability of entire system.
cons: Not sure if this supports video attachment now, last time one of the user in my group talked about it. I believe video attachments (larger size, sometime) should be allowed. Theme modifications and Customization panel (GUI) to admin user could be good things to have.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: A easy to use, non cluttered and simple Issue/Bug tracking system
description: Ease of Use is the main reason why I would go with this application. I also got good support during the period of usage and the support is ready to hear.
author: ✒ pradosh N.
pros: As a CI/CD platofrm developer and architect for software application have used apps like GitHub Issues, BugZilla and Atlassian Jira. The app distinguishes itself by being less cluttered and ease to use. The data and issues are clearly visible in the Dash Board. The Simplicity is its USP, and accordingly hits the sweet spot for most requirements.
cons: Compared to applications like Jira, the functionality is not huge and diverse. Jira is highly customizable and is the gold standard as far the range of functions are concerned in this area. However it has integration with Jira if necessary for complicated requirements. Didn't explore it, as we didn't have those requirements.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Prankit A.
pros: 1. Live Support for the open and closed bugs is looking good. completed/total bug is a good choice for clear understanding. 2. Side navigation is perfectly designed to display the current window to the user. 3. Apps display on the home page on every tab has perfectly UI. 4. On hovering to the app on the homepage, adding the functionality of copying the app link is a good move. 5. Adding an intercom messenger button is good but it would be better if a. It displayed a cross icon instead of a down arrow icon when it is opened. b. user had an option to minimize it instead of displaying it on the page all the time. 6. Displaying gifs according to the functionality when no data is available is a perfect and attractive idea. 7. UI of displaying issues as a list and as a board is well designed.
cons: It may have some more functionalities like writing and reviewing test cases, changing the status of bugs according to their life cycle etc. Also, there are some bugs in the application that I think need to be fixed. 1. On the homepage, the alignment of the icon with the tab text (Private, Public, Contributes Projects) should be the same. 2. On clicking the "Create New Project" button on the homepage a dialog appears. If the user clicks on the information icon displayed after the private toggle, the information window that appeared is overflowing the dialog. 3. Navigation from "Projects" and "My Dashboard" from side navigation is on the same route. If that's the functionality then only 1 should present mentioning both the title. 4. If the user clicks on "Restart Tour" from side navigation then tour functionality should be performed. 5. When a user clicks on the "Manage" option from the side navigation, items in the stacked navigation should be the same all the time.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Virendra K.
pros: I have used this product for about 4 months now and what I like the most is that it keeps getting improved as per our needs. I remember when I started using it, there was only one way integration possible with Jira but then the sync was implemented both ways. It is the most simplest tool I have used so far compared to the other options available. I can have multiple teams inside one tool and also have multiple projects within those teams. The best thing is that I can also bring my clients inside it. Client onboarding is also very easy and flawless. Support people are also very humble. Overall, it's the best bug tracking tool.
cons: Nothing as such to dislike about it. In the recent past I was encountering increase in loading time but it is getting improved as I can see from the social media posts and also from the live support.

review_title: Standardize Bug Reports from both QA and Non-QA testers
author: Antonius M.
pros: Standardize Bug Reports: - It helps structure the thinking and execution of tests, as well as guide novices in producing helpful bug reports without much effort. Moreover, you're using the same tool for browser and mobile-based testing to generate a bug report with an image flow (screencast) from two separate sources. This greatly reduces the friction and dread of yet another bug report when you are testing a buggy sprint/release. - Great on its own and as a JIRA integration - Add-on tool which can help with reporting and managing bugs, but it shouldn't uproot your processes, it's more of something that you can leverage to streamline, set up, recording, and reporting of any testing efforts. - Exploratory testing is where I find many bugs, so being able to quickly report bugs while I'm testing in this manner is appreciated. - Easy workflow, so training doesn't take much time.
cons: Simple overall concept, yet it does require some training (although minimal) to grasp the workflow and main benefits. It doesn't tie back to your test case tool (which will usually have a pass/fail and reporting feature). It that sense you might feel like using either tool but not both. So the question will be which one are you going to drop? - That being said, I've heard of teams abandoning the test case suite to "save time". This always makes me raise my eyebrows, but since there's no abandoning reporting, you'll find bugasura as a preferred go-to tool.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial
